topics.title: Message Authentication
topics.definition: Message authentication ensures that the message comes from the correct sender and was not altered.
topics.explanation: It verifies integrity and authenticity of a message using cryptographic techniques such as keyed hashes or digital signatures.

topics.title: Encryption
topics.definition: Encryption converts plaintext into ciphertext to protect confidentiality.
topics.explanation: It protects data during transmission or storage using symmetric or asymmetric algorithms.

topics.title: Transport Layer Security
topics.definition: TLS secures communication between client and server over an untrusted network.
topics.explanation: It combines encryption, message authentication, and certificate-based identity to provide confidentiality and integrity.
